name: Whole Wheat Flatbread (Chapati)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flours and salt in a large mixing bowl.
2. Mix in oil and water to form a stiff dough, adding more water if necessary.
3. Remove the dough from the bowl and knead on a lightly floured surface until smooth, about 5 minutes.
4. Return the dough to the bowl, cover with a towel, and set aside in a warm place for one hour.
5. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and divide into 14 equal portions. Roll each piece into a ball and cover with a damp towel.
6. Heat an ungreased griddle or large frying pan over medium heat. Working with one ball of dough at a time, flatten it, then roll into a 6-inch circle, using only enough flour to keep the dough from sticking.
7. When the griddle is hot, pick up the dough, shake off excess flour, and place it on the hot pan. Cook until brown spots appear, about one minute.
8. Flip the dough over and cook on the other side. (Chapati may puff up while cooking.)
9. Cover and place in a warm oven while cooking the remaining chapatis.
